title: Audit Finding
description: One row in audit-findings.jsonl emitted by phase 2 of the deep-ai-council release-cleanup packet. Each line is a separate JSON document conforming to this schema.

artifact_path.description: Repo-relative path to the deep-ai-council artifact under audit (e.g. .opencode/skills/deep-ai-council/references/scoring_rubric.md)

template_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the sk-doc template the artifact is compared against (e.g. .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/skill/skill_reference_template.md)

severity.description: P0 = HARD BLOCKER, P1 = required (resolve or get approval), P2 = optional (can defer with rationale)

finding_class.description: Required by checklist CHK-FIX-001. Drives blast-radius assessment.

description.description: Specific deviation description with file:line refs where applicable

evidence.description: Optional list of evidence pointers (file:line, command output snippet, grep result)

candidate_verified.description: For phase-1 candidate-drift items: true once verified against the actual template + file. Findings derived from candidates MUST be verified before an edit (false-positive-P0 discipline). null for findings discovered fresh in phase 2.

status.description: Status at the moment this line was last updated. 'dropped_false_positive' = a candidate that failed phase-2 verification.

resolution_sha.description: Git SHA of the commit that resolved this finding (required when status=resolved)

deferral_rationale.description: Required when status=deferred or status=dropped_false_positive. Explains why the fix is being held or the candidate was dropped.

detected_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the finding was emitted

resolved_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when status transitioned to resolved/deferred/dropped

examples.description: SKILL.md §7 Quality Targets claims 'manual testing package has 18 scenarios across 7 categories' but §3 + README + the directory show 32 scenarios across 9 categories
examples.recommended_fix: Update §7 Quality Targets to '32 scenarios across 9 categories' to match §3 and the on-disk playbook
